Root Cause: The function strEscape is not correctly handling Unicode characters. Specifically, it is not correctly escaping characters in the range of 0x80 to 0xFF.

Suggestion 1: Modify the strEscape function to correctly handle Unicode characters
The function strEscape should be modified to correctly escape Unicode characters in the range of 0x80 to 0xFF. This can be done by adding a new case to the switch statement to handle these characters. The new case should use the Unicode escape sequence (\\uXXXX) to escape these characters.

Suggestion 2: Use a CharsetEncoder to handle Unicode characters
The function strEscape can be modified to use a CharsetEncoder to handle Unicode characters. The CharsetEncoder can be used to determine if a character can be represented in the output charset, and if not, the character can be escaped using the Unicode escape sequence (\\uXXXX).

Suggestion 3: Modify the strEscape function to use a more robust escaping mechanism
The function strEscape can be modified to use a more robust escaping mechanism, such as the one provided by the Java String class. This can be done by using the String.replace() method to replace special characters with their escaped equivalents.
